content: By calibrating the ICM curiosity mechanism to only generate internal rewards for novel observations that fit within an autonomous agent's existing working memory chunk limit of 3–5, we can avoid the common problem of curiosity-driven agents wandering into intractably complex reasoning tasks that exceed their inherent working memory capacity. This bounded curiosity framework would steer agents to explore incrementally novel, learnable patterns rather than getting stuck in unresolvable open-ended complexity.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
